meta.title: AI Compass — Top 100 AI Tools
meta.description: Curated, ranked directory of the top 100 AI tools for 2026. Covers LLMs, coding, image/video generation, research, productivity, voice, design, and more.

tools.name: ChatGPT
tools.tagline: The world's most-used AI assistant with GPT-4o at its core
tools.bestFor: Multimodal reasoning, writing, coding, image gen
tools.studentPerk: 50% discount available at select universities via .edu email
tools.uniHack: Enable web browsing and upload your syllabus — ask it to create a week-by-week study plan with resource links for each module.
tools.description: OpenAI's flagship product and the most widely adopted AI tool globally. GPT-4o delivers multimodal reasoning across text, images, audio, and code. The GPT Store hosts thousands of custom assistants for specialized tasks.

tools.name: Claude
tools.tagline: Constitutional AI for safe, nuanced long-context reasoning
tools.bestFor: Long documents, coding, analysis, creative writing
tools.studentPerk: Free tier includes Claude 3.5 Haiku — powerful for essays, problem sets, and research
tools.uniHack: Use Socratic learning mode: paste lecture notes and ask 'quiz me on this, then explain the parts I miss.' Far more effective than passive re-reading.
tools.description: Anthropic's frontier model family built with Constitutional AI for aligned, safe outputs. Claude 3.7 Sonnet leads in coding benchmarks while Opus 4 dominates long-context analysis. Exceptional at nuanced instruction-following.

tools.name: Gemini
tools.tagline: Google's multimodal AI with a 2M token context window
tools.bestFor: Long-context research, Google Workspace integration
tools.studentPerk: Often included in Google Workspace for Education — check with your university IT
tools.uniHack: Load an entire semester's PDFs into one conversation. Ask it to surface connections between papers, flag contradictions, and draft a literature review outline.
tools.description: Google DeepMind's flagship multimodal model deeply integrated into Google's ecosystem. Gemini 2.0 Ultra holds the record for longest commercially available context window, enabling analysis of entire codebases or book-length documents.

tools.name: GitHub Copilot
tools.tagline: AI pair programmer embedded in every major IDE
tools.bestFor: Code completion, refactoring, PR reviews
tools.studentPerk: 100% FREE for students via GitHub Student Developer Pack — includes Copilot Pro
tools.uniHack: Apply at education.github.com for the Student Developer Pack. Unlocks Copilot Pro, JetBrains IDEs, Azure credits, and 100+ professional tools at zero cost.
tools.description: The market-leading AI coding assistant with deep IDE integration. Copilot's context awareness spans your entire codebase, not just the current file. The 2026 version adds agentic multi-file editing and automated PR review.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.tagline: The AI-first code editor built on VS Code
tools.bestFor: Agentic coding, full-project refactors
tools.studentPerk: Free hobby plan with 2,000 completions/month covers most coursework projects
tools.uniHack: Index your entire coursework repo, then use Agent mode to ask 'implement this algorithm from my lecture notes' — it reads both the code and your notes simultaneously.
tools.description: Cursor reimagines the IDE with AI as a first-class citizen. Its Agent mode can autonomously navigate codebases, edit multiple files, run terminal commands, and self-correct errors — a true autonomous coding collaborator.

tools.name: Midjourney
tools.tagline: The gold standard for artistic AI image generation
tools.bestFor: Artistic imagery, editorial, concept art
tools.studentPerk: No EDU discount — Basic at $10/mo is the most affordable entry point for premium image quality
tools.uniHack: Use --style raw with photographic prompts for stunning editorial imagery in thesis presentations and design briefs. The Basic plan's fast GPU hours cover most project needs.
tools.description: Midjourney has defined the aesthetic standard for AI art. Its V7 model delivers photorealistic and painterly outputs with unrivalled compositional quality. Character reference and style reference features enable consistent branded imagery at scale.

tools.name: Perplexity AI
tools.tagline: Real-time AI research engine with cited sources
tools.bestFor: Research, fact-finding, sourced answers
tools.studentPerk: Perplexity Pro offered free to students at select partner universities — check your institution
tools.uniHack: Set search focus to 'Academic' to surface peer-reviewed papers. Use Spaces to build a shared research environment for group projects with shared memory.
tools.description: Perplexity combines LLM reasoning with real-time web retrieval to produce cited, sourced answers. Its Academic mode searches scholarly databases directly, making it an essential tool for research-heavy workflows.

tools.name: Notion AI
tools.tagline: AI woven natively into your all-in-one workspace
tools.bestFor: Notes, docs, databases, project management
tools.studentPerk: Notion Plus is FREE for students and educators — includes AI credits with verified .edu email
tools.uniHack: Build a 'second brain': link meeting notes, textbook summaries, and flashcard databases with AI auto-tagging. Use AI Q&A to ask questions across all your pages.
tools.description: Notion AI makes the world's most popular all-in-one workspace intelligent. It can read, summarise, and write across your entire knowledge base — the Q&A feature is particularly powerful for research note management.

tools.name: Runway Gen-4
tools.tagline: Cinematic AI video generation with scene continuity
tools.bestFor: Video generation, filmmaking, motion design
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 125 credits/month — enough for several short experimental videos
tools.uniHack: Use for film studies, advertising briefs, or digital arts coursework. Free tier is enough for a compelling 15-second concept video for any creative brief.
tools.description: Runway's Gen-4 model produces cinematic-quality video with consistent characters across scenes — a major leap over prior generations. Widely used in professional film and advertising production pipelines.

tools.name: ElevenLabs
tools.tagline: Hyper-realistic AI voice synthesis in 32 languages
tools.bestFor: Voiceovers, voice cloning, dubbing, TTS
tools.studentPerk: Free tier includes 10,000 characters/month — enough for narrated presentations and short videos
tools.uniHack: Clone your own voice for accessibility materials, narrate slide decks in multiple languages, or create audio summaries of dense readings for on-the-go revision.
tools.description: ElevenLabs delivers the most natural-sounding AI voice synthesis available. Instant voice cloning from a 60-second sample is its signature feature. Used by creators, publishers, game studios, and enterprises at massive scale.

tools.name: Sora
tools.tagline: OpenAI's groundbreaking text-to-video model
tools.pricingDetail: Included in ChatGPT Plus · Pro $200/mo for 4K
tools.bestFor: Realistic video generation, storyboarding
tools.studentPerk: Accessible on ChatGPT Plus at $20/mo — bundled with your existing subscription
tools.uniHack: Use Sora for storyboarding film or animation projects. Generate reference clips to visualise shot compositions before live production.
tools.description: OpenAI's video generation model trained on vast video datasets using diffusion transformers. Sora produces physically plausible, temporally consistent videos that set a new bar for realism in AI-generated content.

tools.name: Stable Diffusion 3.5
tools.tagline: Open-source image generation — run locally, free forever
tools.bestFor: Local deployment, fine-tuning, custom workflows
tools.studentPerk: Completely free to run locally. Use Google Colab free tier for cloud GPU access with no installation needed
tools.uniHack: Run via Google Colab free T4 GPU — no local hardware needed. Perfect for computer vision coursework, diffusion model research papers, and thesis projects on generative AI.
tools.description: The world's most widely deployed open-source image generation model. SD 3.5's distillation improvements enable high-quality outputs in just 4 steps locally. The ecosystem of custom models, LoRAs, and ComfyUI workflows is unmatched.

tools.name: Grammarly
tools.tagline: AI writing assistant for grammar, tone, and clarity
tools.bestFor: Grammar, tone analysis, plagiarism check
tools.studentPerk: Grammarly Education: free or heavily discounted Premium for students through institutional licensing
tools.uniHack: Set tone goals before submitting: 'formal + confident' for academic writing. Target Grade 12 Flesch readability. Run plagiarism check 48 hours before submission to allow rewrites.
tools.description: Grammarly remains the most widely used AI writing assistant with 40M+ daily users. GrammarlyGO adds full generative capabilities while the core product continues to lead in grammar precision and tone analysis.

tools.name: v0
tools.tagline: Prompt-to-production React UI component generation
tools.bestFor: React UI components, Next.js projects, prototyping
tools.studentPerk: Free tier generates full React + shadcn/ui components — covers most student portfolio and coursework projects
tools.uniHack: Build your entire portfolio site in one afternoon with v0: describe each section, export to Next.js, deploy for free on Vercel. Recruiters who inspect source code will be impressed.
tools.description: Vercel's v0 generates production-ready React components from natural language, directly targeting the shadcn/ui design system. The tight Vercel integration means deployment is one click away.

tools.name: Adobe Firefly
tools.tagline: Commercially safe AI image gen inside Creative Cloud
tools.pricingDetail: Free 25 credits/mo · Firefly Standard $9.99/mo · Creative Cloud included
tools.bestFor: Commercial-safe images, Photoshop integration, design
tools.studentPerk: Adobe Creative Cloud for Students at ~60% discount includes Firefly. Check if your university provides free CC access.
tools.uniHack: If your university provides Adobe CC (many do), Firefly credits are included. Use Generative Fill in Photoshop for design coursework — outputs are copyright-safe for commercial use.
tools.description: Adobe Firefly is trained exclusively on licensed Adobe Stock and public domain content, making every output commercially safe. Deep integration into Photoshop's Generative Fill is the most-used AI feature in professional design workflows.

tools.name: Otter.ai
tools.tagline: Real-time AI transcription and meeting intelligence
tools.bestFor: Lecture transcription, meeting notes, action items
tools.studentPerk: Free plan includes 300 min/month transcription — ideal for capturing lecture content
tools.uniHack: Record every lecture, generate transcripts, then ask the AI summary 'What are the 5 most testable concepts from today?' Beats re-reading 80-slide decks for exam prep.
tools.description: Otter.ai's real-time transcription with speaker diarization is the go-to tool for lecture and meeting capture. The AI summary feature distills hour-long meetings into structured action items in under a minute.

tools.name: Kagi
tools.tagline: Ad-free, privacy-first AI-powered search engine
tools.bestFor: Private research, academic search, high signal-to-noise
tools.studentPerk: At $5/mo, cheaper than most textbook chapters. No ads means higher quality results per search.
tools.uniHack: Use the Universal Summarizer on any paper URL for an instant structured breakdown. 'Lenses' restrict searches to academic or technical sources. Bangs send queries directly to 200+ academic databases.
tools.description: Kagi flips the search model: users pay for quality instead of being the product. The result is higher-quality, ad-free results with powerful AI features layered on top. FastGPT and the Universal Summarizer are standout research tools.

tools.name: Grok
tools.tagline: Real-time AI with unfiltered reasoning and X integration
tools.bestFor: Real-time information, unfiltered analysis, X/Twitter data
tools.studentPerk: Included with X Premium at $8/mo — check for student rate through X
tools.uniHack: Use Grok's real-time X data access for media studies, political science research, and social media analysis coursework. Excellent for trend analysis with live data.
tools.description: xAI's Grok model combines frontier reasoning with real-time access to the X platform's data stream. Grok 3's reasoning mode (Think) competes with the top tier while its willingness to discuss edgy topics attracts a distinct audience.

tools.name: Mistral AI
tools.tagline: European frontier models — efficient, open, and capable
tools.pricingDetail: Free API tier · La Plateforme pay-as-you-go
tools.bestFor: Efficiency, multilingual, open-weight deployment
tools.studentPerk: Mistral 7B and Mixtral 8x7B are fully free to run locally — no API costs for research
tools.uniHack: Download Mistral 7B via Ollama and run it completely free on your laptop. Perfect for building NLP coursework projects, RAG systems, and fine-tuning experiments without API costs.
tools.description: Paris-based Mistral produces Europe's leading frontier models. Their efficient Mixture-of-Experts architecture and commitment to open weights makes them the go-to for developers needing capable, locally deployable models.

tools.name: Llama 3.3
tools.tagline: Meta's open-source frontier LLM — free for all
tools.pricingDetail: Free and open weights (commercial use allowed)
tools.bestFor: Local deployment, research, fine-tuning, RAG
tools.studentPerk: Completely free — download and run locally or via HuggingFace. No usage costs ever.
tools.uniHack: Use Ollama to run Llama 3.3 70B locally in minutes. Build a RAG system over your lecture notes and course materials as a capstone or thesis project.
tools.description: Meta's Llama 3.3 is the world's most widely deployed open-source LLM. The 70B model matches GPT-4 level performance on many benchmarks while being free to download, fine-tune, and deploy commercially.

tools.name: Canva AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered design platform for everyone
tools.bestFor: Presentations, social media, documents, brand
tools.studentPerk: Canva for Education is FREE for students and teachers — includes all Pro features
tools.uniHack: Apply for Canva for Education with your .edu email to get Pro free. Use Magic Design + AI Presentations to generate full decks from a topic prompt — then customise.
tools.description: Canva has become the most widely used design platform globally, with AI features woven throughout. Magic Design can generate complete presentations from a single prompt, while the image generation and editing tools rival standalone products.

tools.name: Copilot (Microsoft)
tools.tagline: Microsoft's AI companion across Windows, Office, and Edge
tools.bestFor: Office integration, Windows 11, enterprise productivity
tools.studentPerk: Microsoft 365 Education is free for students at many universities — includes Copilot
tools.uniHack: Check if your university provides free Microsoft 365 Education access. Copilot in Word can draft, summarise, and reformat your essays; Copilot in Excel handles data analysis coursework.
tools.description: Microsoft's Copilot strategy embeds AI into every product surface — from the OS to Office to Edge. Powered by OpenAI's models, Copilot Pro unlocks deep document-aware assistance inside Word, Excel, and Outlook.

tools.name: Bolt.new
tools.tagline: Full-stack web app generation in the browser
tools.bestFor: Full-stack prototyping, React/Node apps, instant deployment
tools.studentPerk: Free tier covers most student app projects with 150K tokens/day
tools.uniHack: Build and demo a full-stack web app for a software engineering course without setting up any local environment. From prompt to deployed URL in under 10 minutes.
tools.description: Bolt.new runs a full Node.js environment in the browser and generates complete, deployable web apps from prompts. Its tight integration with WebContainers eliminates the setup gap between generation and running code.

tools.name: Windsurf
tools.tagline: Agentic AI IDE with 'Cascade' collaborative AI flows
tools.bestFor: Agentic coding, deep codebase awareness
tools.studentPerk: Free tier is genuinely capable for student projects with unlimited code completions
tools.uniHack: Windsurf's Cascade maintains full context of what you've been building across sessions — unlike competitors. Great for semester-long capstone projects with evolving codebases.
tools.description: Codeium's Windsurf IDE differentiates with 'Cascade' — an AI system that maintains deep awareness of your development history and intent. Its free tier is notably generous, making it the default choice for developers not on Cursor Pro.

tools.name: Ideogram 2.0
tools.tagline: AI image generation with best-in-class text rendering
tools.bestFor: Typography in images, logos, posters, marketing
tools.studentPerk: Free tier generates 10 high-quality images per day — more than enough for design coursework
tools.uniHack: Best free tool for creating posters, event flyers, and presentation graphics with accurate text. Ideal for marketing, journalism, and design students who need quick professional visuals.
tools.description: Ideogram 2.0 leads the industry in accurate text rendering within images — a historically difficult problem for diffusion models. Its free tier is generous and the quality-per-dollar ratio is among the best in class.

tools.name: Descript
tools.tagline: Edit video and podcast by editing the text transcript
tools.bestFor: Podcast editing, video content, filler word removal
tools.studentPerk: Free tier supports 1 watermarked export/month — enough for class presentations and coursework
tools.uniHack: Record a presentation, then edit out all 'um', 'uh', and filler words in one click. Create a polished, professional video submission in a fraction of the time.
tools.description: Descript's paradigm-shifting approach lets you edit audio and video by editing the text transcript. Removing filler words, fixing retakes, and rearranging content is as simple as editing a document.

tools.name: Synthesia
tools.tagline: Create AI avatar videos without a camera or studio
tools.bestFor: Training videos, explainers, multilingual content
tools.studentPerk: No student discount, but the Starter plan at $18/mo is affordable for project-based coursework
tools.uniHack: Create multilingual educational videos for international studies or communications coursework. Avatar + script is faster than any camera-based production workflow.
tools.description: Synthesia's AI avatar platform is the B2B standard for corporate training, onboarding, and product explainer videos. Generate a polished presenter video from a script in minutes, in 140+ languages.

tools.name: DeepL
tools.tagline: The most accurate AI translation tool available
tools.bestFor: Document translation, formal/technical text
tools.studentPerk: Free tier translates 3 full documents per month — sufficient for most coursework translation needs
tools.uniHack: Translate research papers from German, French, or Japanese academic literature that aren't available in English. DeepL's accuracy on technical and academic texts far outperforms Google Translate.
tools.description: DeepL consistently outperforms competitors in blind translation quality tests, especially for European languages. Its nuanced understanding of formal register and technical vocabulary makes it the tool of choice for professional translators.

tools.name: Pika
tools.tagline: Creative AI video generation for everyone
tools.bestFor: Short-form video, social content, creative experiments
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 150 credits/month — enough for multiple short video experiments
tools.uniHack: Use Pika's 'Pikaffects' for creative arts and media coursework. The free plan comfortably covers a semester's worth of short-form video projects.
tools.description: Pika Labs positioned itself as the accessible, creative-first video generation tool. Pikaffects — viral style transformations like 'melt' and 'inflate' — drove explosive social media adoption and define Pika's creative identity.

tools.name: Jasper AI
tools.tagline: Enterprise AI content platform for marketing teams
tools.bestFor: Marketing copy, brand voice, content at scale
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — ChatGPT or Claude serve the same use cases at $20/mo
tools.uniHack: If studying marketing, advertising, or communications, use the free trial to generate a portfolio of brand campaigns. The Brand Voice feature teaches you how to systematise tone-of-voice.
tools.description: Jasper is the leading enterprise content platform for marketing teams, with a focus on brand voice consistency across large content operations. Its template library and campaign workflow tools are built for agency-scale production.

tools.name: Replit AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered browser IDE for learning and building
tools.bestFor: Learning to code, quick prototypes, CS education
tools.studentPerk: Replit offers educational pricing and free teams for Edu classrooms
tools.uniHack: Run Python, JS, or any of 50+ languages in the browser with zero setup. Use as a collaborative coding environment for group assignments — everyone edits live like Google Docs.
tools.description: Replit combines a powerful browser-based IDE with an AI agent that can generate entire applications from descriptions. Its zero-setup model makes it the default for CS education and beginner-friendly coding environments.

tools.name: Luma AI (Dream Machine)
tools.tagline: High-quality 3D and video generation from images
tools.bestFor: 3D capture, smooth video generation, product viz
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 30 video generations per month — excellent for coursework and portfolio building
tools.uniHack: Architecture, product design, and 3D modelling students can use Luma's NeRF capture to create photorealistic 3D scenes from iPhone videos for digital portfolios.
tools.description: Luma AI is known for the smoothest motion and physics of any video generation model. Dream Machine's cinematic output quality and Luma's 3D NeRF capture technology serve both creators and 3D professionals.

tools.name: Anthropic Claude API
tools.tagline: Access frontier Claude models directly via API
tools.bestFor: Building AI apps, enterprise, document processing
tools.studentPerk: No formal student pricing — use Claude.ai free tier for personal use; API is for building apps
tools.uniHack: Build your capstone AI project using the Claude API. Anthropic's student startup program may provide free credits — check anthropic.com/startups.
tools.description: Anthropic's Claude API gives developers direct access to the Claude model family with industry-leading long-context performance and safety. The extended thinking feature in Claude 3.7 Sonnet is state-of-the-art for complex reasoning tasks.

tools.name: Figma AI
tools.tagline: AI-accelerated product design inside Figma
tools.bestFor: UI design, prototyping, design systems
tools.studentPerk: Figma Education plan is FREE for students and educators — full Professional features
tools.uniHack: Apply for Figma Education at figma.com/education. Use AI First Draft to generate wireframes from prompts, then refine in the full Figma editor. Standard for UX/product design coursework.
tools.description: Figma is the industry standard for collaborative UI/UX design. The 2025 AI upgrade adds AI-generated wireframes, one-click mockup generation, and intelligent component suggestions that dramatically accelerate the early design phase.

tools.name: Framer AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered website builder with code-quality output
tools.bestFor: Portfolio websites, landing pages, interactive design
tools.studentPerk: Free plan includes publishing on framer.site subdomain — perfect for student portfolios
tools.uniHack: Generate a portfolio site from a text prompt in under 5 minutes with Framer AI. The Mini plan at $5/mo adds a custom domain. Recruiters consistently rate Framer portfolios higher than template-built ones.
tools.description: Framer bridges the gap between design tools and website builders. Its AI can generate complete, animated websites from a text description. The output quality and interaction design capabilities are unmatched in the no-code space.

tools.name: Wolfram Alpha
tools.tagline: Computational knowledge engine for math and science
tools.bestFor: Mathematics, physics, chemistry, STEM problem-solving
tools.studentPerk: Wolfram Pro Student at $7.25/mo is the most powerful STEM problem-solver at this price point
tools.uniHack: Use step-by-step solutions to understand HOW to solve problems — don't just copy answers. Wolfram's natural language input means you can paste equations from your textbook verbatim.
tools.description: Wolfram Alpha remains the gold standard for computational knowledge, covering mathematics, physics, chemistry, engineering, and statistics. Its step-by-step solutions and Wolfram Language notebooks are indispensable for STEM students.

tools.name: Elicit
tools.tagline: AI research assistant for academic literature
tools.bestFor: Literature reviews, paper analysis, systematic reviews
tools.studentPerk: Free tier provides 5,000 credits monthly — enough for several literature review sessions
tools.uniHack: Upload your research question and Elicit will find, rank, and extract key claims from 100+ papers simultaneously. Cuts literature review time from weeks to hours for thesis students.
tools.description: Elicit automates the most time-consuming parts of academic research. Its semantic search finds relevant papers even without keyword matches, and the extraction tables pull structured data from dozens of papers simultaneously.

tools.name: Consensus
tools.tagline: Evidence-based AI search over 200M academic papers
tools.bestFor: Evidence-based research, scientific fact-checking
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 20 AI searches/month over 200M papers — often enough for essay research
tools.uniHack: Ask yes/no research questions ('Does caffeine improve cognitive performance?') and Consensus synthesises the scientific consensus from peer-reviewed sources. Perfect for evidence-based essays.
tools.description: Consensus is purpose-built for scientific evidence retrieval. Its 'Consensus Meter' shows what percentage of papers support or refute a claim, giving researchers an immediate sense of the scientific landscape on any question.

tools.name: Tome
tools.tagline: AI-native presentation and storytelling tool
tools.bestFor: AI-generated presentations, storytelling, pitches
tools.studentPerk: Free tier includes unlimited AI-generated presentations — more than enough for coursework
tools.uniHack: Generate a full presentation outline and slide content from a single prompt in under 2 minutes. Use as a starting structure, then customise — faster than building from a blank PowerPoint.
tools.description: Tome reimagines the presentation as an AI-native artifact. Its generative AI can produce a complete, designed presentation with relevant images from a single topic prompt, establishing a new workflow baseline for slide creation.

tools.name: Gamma
tools.tagline: Beautiful AI-generated decks, docs, and webpages
tools.bestFor: Presentations, reports, one-pagers, proposals
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 400 AI credits — enough for 4-5 complete presentations for coursework
tools.uniHack: Paste your essay or report into Gamma and ask it to convert to a presentation. The output is surprisingly polished and takes 30 seconds vs 2 hours of manual slide building.
tools.description: Gamma produces the most visually polished AI-generated presentations of any tool in its category. The paste-to-presentation workflow (turn a document into a deck) is its most-used feature among business and academic users.

tools.name: Lovable
tools.tagline: Chat to build production full-stack web apps
tools.bestFor: No-code app building, React + Supabase apps
tools.studentPerk: Free tier allows 5 conversations/day — enough for learning and simple projects
tools.uniHack: Build a full-stack app with authentication, database, and UI for a software engineering course project without writing backend code. Lovable generates React + Supabase by default.
tools.description: Lovable (formerly GPT Engineer) builds production-quality full-stack apps through conversation. Its React + Supabase default stack and GitHub sync make it the most production-ready AI app builder available.

tools.name: Eleven Reader
tools.tagline: AI-powered reading app with natural voice narration
tools.bestFor: Reading articles, PDFs, e-books aloud
tools.studentPerk: Free app that turns any PDF, article, or document into a podcast-quality audio experience
tools.uniHack: Import lecture notes, research papers, and textbook chapters. Listen while commuting, exercising, or doing chores — maximise passive study time without screen fatigue.
tools.description: Eleven Reader transforms any text into a natural, podcast-quality audio experience using ElevenLabs' industry-leading voice synthesis. It's the highest-quality text-to-speech reading app currently available.

tools.name: Gemini Code Assist
tools.tagline: Google's AI coding assistant for IDEs and Google Cloud
tools.bestFor: Google Cloud development, Java, Python, Go
tools.studentPerk: Free individual plan — apply for Google Cloud credits for Students for additional API access
tools.uniHack: Combine free Gemini Code Assist with $300 Google Cloud free credits (new accounts) to build and host a full AI application for capstone projects.
tools.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist integrates with popular IDEs and is deeply aware of Google Cloud APIs and services. The free individual tier makes it a compelling Copilot alternative, particularly for GCP-heavy development.

tools.name: Kling AI
tools.tagline: Chinese frontier video generation with cinematic quality
tools.bestFor: Cinematic video generation, high resolution
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 66 credits daily — one of the most generous free tiers in AI video generation
tools.uniHack: The free tier alone is sufficient for film and media arts coursework. Generate high-quality cinematic clips at 1080p without any subscription.
tools.description: Kling AI from Kuaishou delivers cinematic video generation quality that competes directly with Runway and Sora. Its exceptionally generous free tier has driven rapid adoption among creators and filmmakers globally.

tools.name: Hugging Face
tools.tagline: The GitHub of AI — 500K+ models, datasets, and spaces
tools.bestFor: Research, model deployment, NLP, fine-tuning
tools.studentPerk: Completely free to access 500K+ models, datasets, and run Spaces demos. Essential for AI/ML coursework.
tools.uniHack: Use HuggingFace Spaces to demo your ML models for free — hosts Gradio/Streamlit apps on shared GPU. Essential for submitting interactive AI coursework demos without server costs.
tools.description: Hugging Face is the central hub for the open-source AI community. Its transformers library is used in virtually every academic AI paper. Spaces provide free GPU hosting for model demos, making it the backbone of AI research sharing.

tools.name: Replicate
tools.tagline: Run any open-source AI model via API in seconds
tools.bestFor: Running open-source models without GPU infrastructure
tools.studentPerk: Pay-as-you-go model means small experiments cost pennies. New accounts get free credits.
tools.uniHack: Run Stable Diffusion, Llama, Whisper, or any of 50K+ models via one API call. Perfect for AI research projects and coursework requiring GPU-intensive models without owning hardware.
tools.description: Replicate provides a unified API for running any of 50,000+ open-source AI models without managing GPU infrastructure. Its consistent interface makes switching between models trivial for developers and researchers.

tools.name: Whisper / WhisperX
tools.tagline: Best-in-class open-source speech recognition
tools.pricingDetail: 100% free and open source (via OpenAI API: $0.006/min)
tools.bestFor: Transcription, subtitles, multilingual audio processing
tools.studentPerk: Free to run locally; OpenAI API version at $0.006/min means a 1-hour lecture costs $0.36 to transcribe
tools.uniHack: Transcribe any lecture recording for $0 (local) or fractions of a cent. WhisperX adds speaker diarization so you can identify who said what — useful for interview-based research.
tools.description: OpenAI's Whisper model is the most accurate open-source speech recognition system, supporting 99 languages. WhisperX adds word-level timestamps and speaker diarization. The standard for offline and privacy-sensitive transcription.

tools.name: Lobe Chat
tools.tagline: Self-hostable, open-source multi-model chat interface
tools.pricingDetail: Free and open source (self-host for free)
tools.bestFor: Privacy-focused LLM chat, multi-model comparison, self-hosting
tools.studentPerk: Completely free to self-host — use your own API keys to get ChatGPT/Claude quality at API cost
tools.uniHack: Deploy on a free Vercel account and use your own API keys. Total cost: OpenAI API charges only, with no platform markup. Compare models side-by-side for AI coursework research.
tools.description: Lobe Chat is the leading open-source alternative to Claude.ai and ChatGPT web interfaces. Its plugin system, multi-model support, and privacy-first self-hosting make it the preferred interface for power users and developers.

tools.name: Phind
tools.tagline: AI search engine built for developers and coders
tools.bestFor: Technical problem-solving, coding questions, debugging
tools.studentPerk: Free tier handles most coding questions — better than Stack Overflow for complex debugging
tools.uniHack: Use Phind instead of Google for any programming question. Its code-aware search synthesises StackOverflow, GitHub, and documentation into a single clean answer with working code examples.
tools.description: Phind is a search engine designed from the ground up for developers. It synthesises information from documentation, GitHub, and StackOverflow into comprehensive, cited answers with ready-to-use code examples.

tools.name: Ollama
tools.tagline: Run LLMs locally on your Mac, Linux, or Windows machine
tools.bestFor: Running LLMs locally, privacy, offline AI, development
tools.studentPerk: Free — run Llama 3.3, Mistral, Phi-3, and 100+ models on your laptop at zero cost
tools.uniHack: Run `ollama run llama3.3` and get a free, private ChatGPT-quality model on your laptop in 3 minutes. No API costs, no internet required, no usage limits. Game-changer for AI coursework and projects.
tools.description: Ollama makes running open-source LLMs locally as simple as `ollama run llama3.3`. Its OpenAI-compatible API means any application built for ChatGPT can switch to local, free, private inference in minutes.

tools.name: Suno AI
tools.tagline: Generate full songs with vocals from a text prompt
tools.bestFor: Music generation, songwriting, creative projects
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 50 credits/day — enough for 10 full songs daily, more than any student project requires
tools.uniHack: Music composition, advertising, and film production students can use Suno to prototype soundtracks, jingles, and background scores for media projects in minutes rather than hours.
tools.description: Suno generates complete songs with professional-quality vocals and instrumentation from a text prompt in about 30 seconds. The v4 model's audio quality and emotional expressiveness have set a new standard for AI music generation.

tools.name: Udio
tools.tagline: Studio-quality AI music generation with fine control
tools.bestFor: Music production, custom stems, genre blending
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 1,200 credits/month — among the most generous in AI music generation
tools.uniHack: Film scoring, audio production, and creative arts students can use Udio's tag-based control to generate genre-specific tracks for assignments. Custom lyrics mode enables full song production workflows.
tools.description: Udio competes directly with Suno with a focus on audio quality and production flexibility. Its granular tag-based controls give musicians more creative agency over the final output — the preferred choice for producers.

tools.name: Mem AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered knowledge management that organises itself
tools.bestFor: Personal knowledge management, note-taking, memory
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — Notion AI on the free student plan is a stronger value alternative
tools.uniHack: Mem's AI automatically links related notes across sessions — useful for connecting ideas across different courses throughout a semester without manual tagging.
tools.description: Mem's key differentiator is AI that automatically surfaces and links related notes without requiring manual organisation. Its semantic search finds relevant past notes even when you don't remember the exact words you used.

tools.name: Reclaim AI
tools.tagline: AI scheduling assistant that defends deep work time
tools.bestFor: Calendar automation, focus time, task scheduling
tools.studentPerk: Free tier includes 1 scheduling habit — enough to auto-schedule daily study blocks
tools.uniHack: Automatically block 'deep study' time around your class schedule and protect it from meetings. Reclaim reschedules automatically when conflicts arise — essential for exam season.
tools.description: Reclaim AI automatically schedules your tasks and habits into your calendar around existing commitments. Its 'defend focus time' feature prevents fragmentation of deep work blocks — a major productivity multiplier for knowledge workers.

tools.name: Speechify
tools.tagline: Speed-read anything — AI voice turns text into audio
tools.bestFor: Speed reading, accessibility, audio learning
tools.studentPerk: Speechify offers 50% student discount. Essential for dyslexia, ADHD, and high-volume reading coursework.
tools.uniHack: Import entire textbooks and listen at 2-3x speed. Most students can absorb content 3-4x faster through Speechify than traditional reading. Transforms a 3-hour reading assignment into 45 minutes.
tools.description: Speechify is the most widely used text-to-speech app with over 10M active users. Its speed training feature helps users work up to 900+ words per minute while maintaining comprehension — particularly valuable for students with high reading loads.

tools.name: Copilot in Excel / Data Analysis
tools.tagline: Natural language data analysis inside Excel
tools.bestFor: Data analysis, formula generation, pivot tables
tools.studentPerk: Check if your university provides free Microsoft 365 Education — includes Copilot in Excel
tools.uniHack: Ask Copilot in natural language: 'Highlight all cells where revenue dropped more than 10% month-over-month.' Replaces hours of formula lookup for data analysis coursework.
tools.description: Copilot in Microsoft Excel enables natural language interaction with spreadsheet data. Ask questions, generate complex formulas, create visualisations, and get data insights without needing to know advanced Excel functions.

tools.name: Cohere
tools.tagline: Enterprise LLM platform for search and RAG
tools.bestFor: Enterprise RAG, semantic search, document processing
tools.studentPerk: Free trial API credits available — sufficient for course projects and research
tools.uniHack: Use Cohere's free Embed API to build a semantic search engine over your course notes — a strong NLP/AI capstone project that's genuinely useful for exam prep.
tools.description: Cohere focuses on enterprise search and RAG applications, with industry-leading embedding and reranking models. Command R+ is optimised for multi-step tool use in agentic RAG pipelines used in large-scale document processing.

tools.name: AI21 Jamba
tools.tagline: Hybrid SSM-Transformer model with 256K context
tools.bestFor: Long document processing, efficient inference
tools.studentPerk: Free API credits for research and development
tools.uniHack: Jamba's efficient architecture makes it cost-effective for processing large volumes of academic papers in research automation projects.
tools.description: AI21's Jamba combines Mamba SSM and Transformer architectures to achieve both long-context capability (256K tokens) and efficient inference — addressing the speed-context tradeoff that plagues pure Transformer models.

tools.name: Wix ADI
tools.tagline: AI-generated websites in minutes, no code needed
tools.bestFor: Small business sites, portfolios, landing pages
tools.studentPerk: Free plan with wix.com subdomain works for course projects; no credit card required
tools.uniHack: Build a course portfolio site or small business project for entrepreneurship coursework in under 20 minutes using Wix ADI — describe your site and it generates the full structure.
tools.description: Wix's AI Design Intelligence creates complete, professionally designed websites from a short description. As the most widely used website builder globally, Wix ADI lowers the creation barrier to near-zero for non-technical users.

tools.name: Writesonic
tools.tagline: AI writing platform with real-time SEO integration
tools.bestFor: SEO content, marketing copy, blog posts
tools.studentPerk: Free tier provides 25 credits monthly — enough for several marketing assignments
tools.uniHack: Marketing and communications students: use Writesonic's SEO mode to learn how professional content writers structure articles for search ranking — directly applicable in digital marketing coursework.
tools.description: Writesonic combines AI writing with deep SEO tooling. Its integration with SurferSEO enables writers to generate content that is simultaneously well-written and optimised for search rankings from the first draft.

tools.name: Copyleaks
tools.tagline: AI content detection and plagiarism checker
tools.bestFor: AI content detection, academic integrity, plagiarism
tools.studentPerk: Check your own work before submitting — free tier scans 10 pages/month
tools.uniHack: Scan your essays through Copyleaks before submission to catch any accidental plagiarism or AI content flags. Understand what triggers detection to write more originally.
tools.description: Copyleaks offers both plagiarism detection and AI content detection, with claims of 99.1% accuracy in detecting GPT-4, Claude, and other model outputs. Widely used by universities and publishers to maintain content integrity.

tools.name: Lensa AI
tools.tagline: AI photo editor and avatar generator
tools.bestFor: Portrait enhancement, AI avatars, background removal
tools.studentPerk: Free tier covers basic photo editing; paid at $7.99/mo for AI avatar generation (Magic Avatars)
tools.uniHack: Use for professional-quality LinkedIn and portfolio headshots without a photographer. Magic Avatars generates 50+ professional portraits from 10–20 selfies.
tools.description: Lensa AI's Magic Avatars feature went viral in 2022 for generating studio-quality portrait variations from a handful of selfies. The app combines professional photo editing features with AI-powered portrait enhancement.

tools.name: Liner AI
tools.tagline: AI research assistant for highlighting and summarising web content
tools.bestFor: Web research, paper highlighting, source management
tools.studentPerk: Free plan supports AI summaries of web pages and papers — excellent for essay research
tools.uniHack: Highlight key passages in papers, ask AI to explain complex sections, and build a sourced research library from a Chrome extension — no copy-pasting into notes apps.
tools.description: Liner combines web highlighting with AI summarisation directly in the browser. Its AI can explain, summarise, and answer questions about any web page or PDF, making it an efficient research companion for students and knowledge workers.

tools.name: Poe by Quora
tools.tagline: One subscription, all frontier AI models
tools.bestFor: Accessing multiple models, model comparison, bot creation
tools.studentPerk: Free tier allows limited access to GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, and others — great for comparison without individual subscriptions
tools.uniHack: Test the same prompt on GPT-4o, Claude, and Gemini simultaneously from one interface. Useful for AI comparison coursework or finding which model performs best for your specific task.
tools.description: Poe gives users access to every major AI model from a single interface with one subscription. It's particularly popular among users who need to compare model responses or use different models for different tasks.

tools.name: Zapier AI
tools.tagline: No-code AI automation connecting 6,000+ apps
tools.bestFor: Workflow automation, app integrations, AI-powered Zaps
tools.studentPerk: Free tier handles 100 tasks/month — enough for basic automation experiments
tools.uniHack: Automate repetitive academic admin: new paper alert → summarise with AI → save to Notion. Or: form response → generate personalised email reply. Build these workflows as portfolio projects.
tools.description: Zapier remains the dominant no-code automation platform. Its AI layer allows creating automation workflows in plain English, and AI Actions enable LLMs to trigger Zapier workflows in real time — a powerful agentic integration primitive.

tools.name: Make (Integromat)
tools.tagline: Visual workflow automation more powerful than Zapier
tools.bestFor: Complex automation, data transformation, AI pipelines
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 1,000 operations/month — significantly more generous than Zapier for complex workflows
tools.uniHack: More powerful than Zapier for complex multi-step data workflows. Build AI pipelines (scrape → process with LLM → output to database) as portfolio projects demonstrating data engineering skills.
tools.description: Make (formerly Integromat) offers more sophisticated workflow logic than Zapier with better data transformation capabilities and a more powerful free tier. Its visual scenario builder makes complex multi-step automations intuitive to design.

tools.name: Lex
tools.tagline: AI-powered writing app for long-form writers
tools.bestFor: Long-form writing, essays, journalism, creative writing
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — Claude or ChatGPT serve most of the same use cases at comparable or lower cost
tools.uniHack: Use the distraction-free writing environment for thesis and dissertation drafts. The AI 'autocomplete' mode helps break writer's block by continuing your prose when you're stuck.
tools.description: Lex is a focused writing environment built specifically for long-form writers. Unlike general productivity tools, Lex's minimal interface and context-aware AI autocomplete are designed to support the craft of extended writing.

tools.name: Gamma AI (Science)
tools.tagline: AI-powered scientific manuscript and data analysis
tools.bestFor: Scientific writing, data interpretation, lab research
tools.studentPerk: Most universities provide institutional Elsevier ScienceDirect access — check your library portal
tools.uniHack: Use your university library's ScienceDirect AI features to summarise papers, extract key findings, and build literature maps across an entire research domain in minutes.
tools.description: Elsevier's AI layer on ScienceDirect transforms the world's largest scientific database into an intelligent research platform. Institutional subscribers get AI summarisation and synthesis tools across 18M+ peer-reviewed articles.

tools.name: Bardeen
tools.tagline: AI browser automation — automate repetitive web tasks
tools.bestFor: Web scraping, data collection, workflow automation
tools.studentPerk: Free plan covers most student automation tasks with unlimited non-cloud automations
tools.uniHack: Automate data collection for research: describe 'scrape all paper titles and authors from this Google Scholar search' and Bardeen builds the automation. Saves hours of manual data entry.
tools.description: Bardeen automates browser-based workflows using AI and no-code building blocks. Its Magic Box feature creates automations from natural language descriptions — powerful for research data collection and repetitive web tasks.

tools.name: Stability AI API
tools.tagline: API access to Stable Diffusion and creative AI models
tools.bestFor: Bulk image generation, custom pipelines, app integration
tools.studentPerk: Small-scale research projects cost pennies per image. New accounts receive free credits.
tools.uniHack: Use the API to build a custom image generation pipeline for computer vision coursework or as a component of an AI capstone project without hosting GPU infrastructure.
tools.description: Stability AI's REST API provides programmatic access to their image, video, and audio generation models. It's the most developer-friendly way to integrate Stable Diffusion into products without managing GPU infrastructure.

tools.name: Dooly
tools.tagline: AI note-taker that syncs with Salesforce in real time
tools.bestFor: Sales notes, CRM sync, meeting summaries
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — primarily a B2B sales tool
tools.uniHack: Business and sales management students can use the free trial to understand CRM automation workflows — valuable industry knowledge for sales and business development career paths.
tools.description: Dooly eliminates the friction between meeting notes and CRM updates. Its AI captures meeting notes and automatically syncs relevant data to Salesforce fields, removing the manual data entry burden from sales teams.

tools.name: Beautiful.ai
tools.tagline: AI-powered presentation design that auto-formats
tools.bestFor: Professional presentations, pitch decks, reports
tools.studentPerk: No student plan — Canva Education (free) is a better alternative for most student presentations
tools.uniHack: Try the free trial for a polished pitch deck for entrepreneurship or business coursework. Beautiful.ai's smart slides auto-reformat as you add content — no manual positioning.
tools.description: Beautiful.ai's smart templates automatically reformat and balance layouts as content is added or removed — eliminating the pixel-pushing that wastes hours in PowerPoint. DesignerBot can generate complete presentations from an outline.

tools.name: Tavus
tools.tagline: AI video personalisation and digital twin platform
tools.bestFor: Personalised video at scale, digital twin, cloning
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — primarily an enterprise B2B video tool
tools.uniHack: Film and media technology students can explore Tavus' API documentation and free trial to understand the architecture of real-time avatar generation for research projects on synthetic media.
tools.description: Tavus is building the infrastructure for conversational AI avatars and personalised video at scale. Its Conversational Video Interface (CVI) enables real-time interactive avatar conversations — a foundational technology for AI-human interaction.

tools.name: Rewind AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered memory for everything you've seen or said
tools.bestFor: Personal memory, meeting recall, knowledge retrieval
tools.studentPerk: No student pricing — significant privacy considerations mean it's not suitable for all users
tools.uniHack: Record all your lecture content and search it later with natural language: 'What did the professor say about the equilibrium formula in the week 4 lecture?' Potentially powerful but privacy-sensitive.
features: Full text search across all content
tools.description: Rewind records your entire digital life locally and makes it searchable with AI. All processing happens on-device, so the data never leaves your machine. A controversial but powerful tool for those comfortable with the privacy tradeoffs.

tools.name: Bing Image Creator
tools.tagline: Free DALL·E 3 image generation inside Bing and Edge
tools.pricingDetail: Free with Microsoft account (115 fast boosts/week)
tools.bestFor: Free high-quality image generation without subscription
tools.studentPerk: Completely free — DALL·E 3 quality images with just a Microsoft account
tools.uniHack: Best free DALL·E 3 access available. Use for presentation graphics, course project visuals, and design briefs without any subscription. 115 fast generations per week is generous for most use cases.
tools.description: Microsoft's Bing Image Creator provides free access to DALL·E 3 quality image generation — the same model underlying ChatGPT Plus image creation. The most accessible high-quality free image generation available with just a Microsoft account.

tools.name: Adobe Podcast
tools.tagline: AI audio enhancement — studio quality from any microphone
tools.pricingDetail: Free (beta) · Included with Adobe Creative Cloud
tools.bestFor: Podcast audio cleanup, voice clarity, background noise removal
tools.studentPerk: Free beta access — no subscription required for the core Enhance Speech feature
tools.uniHack: Record a presentation or assignment on your laptop microphone, then run it through Adobe Podcast Enhance Speech. Output sounds like a professional studio recording. Game-changer for video submissions.
tools.description: Adobe Podcast's Enhance Speech feature performs near-magical audio cleanup — removing background noise, reverb, and recording artifacts to produce studio-quality voice audio from any microphone. Currently free in beta.

tools.name: Loom AI
tools.tagline: AI video messaging for async communication
tools.bestFor: Async video updates, project explainers, team communication
tools.studentPerk: Loom Education is free for students and educators — unlimited videos up to 5 minutes
tools.uniHack: Record video project presentations and submit asynchronously. Loom AI auto-generates transcripts, summaries, and chapters — making long project videos much easier for professors to review.
tools.description: Loom's AI layer adds auto-transcription, AI summaries, and filler word removal to its async video platform. The Loom Education free tier makes it widely used for academic project presentations and course content delivery.

tools.name: Tabnine
tools.tagline: Privacy-first AI code completion with local model option
tools.bestFor: Privacy-conscious coding, local AI completion, all IDEs
tools.studentPerk: Free tier provides AI completions across all supported IDEs — good alternative to Copilot for privacy-sensitive codework
tools.uniHack: If your coursework involves proprietary or sensitive code, Tabnine's local model option keeps your code entirely private — unlike cloud-based alternatives.
tools.description: Tabnine differentiates with privacy-first code completion, offering a fully local model that never sends code to external servers. The enterprise version learns from team codebases while keeping all data within corporate infrastructure.

tools.name: HeyGen
tools.tagline: Create realistic AI avatar videos at scale
tools.bestFor: Avatar videos, video translation, marketing content
tools.studentPerk: Free tier includes 1 video per month — sufficient for a single high-quality project video
tools.uniHack: Create a polished video assignment from a script without filming. HeyGen's Video Translation can also dub existing videos into 40+ languages for international studies coursework.
tools.description: HeyGen's video translation feature went viral for producing convincing multilingual versions of any video with accurate lip sync. It's now one of the most widely used tools for marketing localisation and content creation at scale.

tools.name: Dataiku
tools.tagline: Enterprise AI/ML platform for data teams
tools.bestFor: Data science, ML pipelines, team collaboration
tools.studentPerk: Dataiku has an Academic program offering free licenses for students — apply at dataiku.com/academia
tools.uniHack: Apply for the Dataiku Academic license for an industry-standard ML platform in your portfolio. Completing Dataiku certifications (free for students) is recognised by data science employers.
tools.description: Dataiku is the leading enterprise AI/ML platform for cross-functional data teams. Its visual pipeline builder and AutoML features make it accessible to business analysts while its Python/R integration satisfies data scientists.

tools.name: Coze
tools.tagline: No-code AI agent and chatbot building platform
tools.pricingDetail: Free generous tier · Team plans available
tools.bestFor: Building AI chatbots, agents, and workflows without code
tools.studentPerk: Very generous free tier — build unlimited bots and publish to multiple platforms at no cost
tools.uniHack: Build a course-specific study assistant bot using your notes and textbook content as a knowledge base. Publish it for free on Discord or web for classmates. Excellent portfolio demonstration of AI agent development.
tools.description: ByteDance's Coze is a powerful no-code platform for building AI agents, bots, and automated workflows. Its generous free tier and simple interface have driven rapid adoption, particularly for building domain-specific assistants.

tools.name: Flux by Black Forest Labs
tools.tagline: State-of-the-art open-weight image generation model
tools.bestFor: High-fidelity image generation, photorealism, compositing
tools.studentPerk: FLUX.1 [schnell] is free for local use — run on consumer hardware with ComfyUI for unlimited free generations
tools.uniHack: FLUX.1 Dev via HuggingFace Spaces is free to try. For local use, the schnell model runs on 8GB VRAM GPUs. Produces state-of-the-art photorealism for design and media coursework.
tools.description: Black Forest Labs' FLUX models immediately established new benchmarks for image quality upon release. FLUX.1 Pro's photorealism and prompt adherence surpassed Midjourney and DALL·E 3, while the open-weight schnell model runs locally.

tools.name: NotebookLM
tools.tagline: AI research notebook that reasons over your uploaded sources
tools.bestFor: Research synthesis, studying uploaded documents, audio overviews
tools.studentPerk: Completely free — one of the best free AI tools available for students
tools.uniHack: Upload all your course materials and generate a custom 'Audio Overview' podcast of any topic — two AI hosts discuss and debate the material. Phenomenal for passive revision during commutes.
tools.description: Google's NotebookLM is the most impressive free AI research tool of 2024-2026. Its Audio Overview feature generates a natural podcast conversation about your uploaded documents — transforming static notes into engaging audio content perfect for studying.

tools.name: Anki with AI Plugins
tools.tagline: Spaced repetition flashcards enhanced with AI generation
tools.bestFor: Medical school, language learning, exam preparation
tools.studentPerk: Free on desktop and Android. AI plugins (AnkiConnect + GPT-4) can auto-generate flashcards from your notes.
tools.uniHack: Install the AnkiConnect and GPT-4 generation add-ons. Paste any lecture notes and auto-generate 50 high-quality flashcards in seconds. The most evidence-based study method combined with the most powerful AI generation.
tools.description: Anki's spaced repetition algorithm is the most evidence-backed study method available. Community AI plugins enable automatic flashcard generation from any text, combining the best learning science with modern AI to dramatically accelerate memorisation.

tools.name: Quizlet AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered flashcards and study tools for students
tools.bestFor: Flashcards, practice tests, study guides
tools.studentPerk: Free tier is genuinely capable with AI-generated study sets and practice tests
tools.uniHack: Paste any lecture notes or chapter text into Quizlet AI to instantly generate a full flashcard set with practice tests and Learn mode. 40M shared sets means most standard courses already have community decks.
tools.description: Quizlet's AI upgrade adds automatic flashcard generation from any text, a conversational AI tutor (Q-Chat), and adaptive practice that focuses on weak areas. The world's most widely used student study platform now has a capable AI layer.

tools.name: Woebot
tools.tagline: AI-powered mental health chatbot based on CBT
tools.bestFor: Mental health support, CBT techniques, emotional check-ins
tools.studentPerk: Free access to evidence-based CBT tools. Not a replacement for professional mental health support.
tools.uniHack: Use for low-stakes mental health check-ins, CBT-based anxiety management tools, and mood tracking during high-stress academic periods like exam season. Always seek professional help for serious concerns.
tools.description: Woebot is the most widely studied AI mental health tool, with clinical research backing its CBT-based approach to managing anxiety and low mood. It provides accessible, evidence-based emotional support between professional therapy sessions.

tools.name: Luminar Neo
tools.tagline: AI-powered photo editing for photographers
tools.bestFor: Photography editing, sky replacement, portrait retouching
tools.studentPerk: Skylum offers student discounts — check their website for current EDU pricing
tools.uniHack: Photography students: Luminar Neo's AI sky replacement and portrait tools save hours of manual Photoshop work. The one-time purchase model is more economical than Adobe CC for single-app needs.
tools.description: Luminar Neo packages the most commonly needed AI photo editing capabilities — sky replacement, portrait retouching, subject masking — into an accessible interface that sits between basic consumer apps and professional Photoshop workflows.

tools.name: Sudowrite
tools.tagline: AI writing partner built specifically for fiction writers
tools.bestFor: Fiction writing, novel development, creative writing
tools.studentPerk: Hobby plan at $10/mo is accessible for creative writing students
tools.uniHack: Creative writing and literature students can use Sudowrite's Describe and Brainstorm tools to explore narrative possibilities and overcome writer's block in fiction coursework.
tools.description: Sudowrite is designed exclusively for fiction writers, with tools that understand narrative craft rather than just grammar. Its Describe mode generates rich sensory details for scenes, while Canvas provides visual story structure management.

tools.name: Reflect
tools.tagline: AI-powered note-taking with networked thought
tools.bestFor: Networked notes, knowledge management, daily notes
tools.studentPerk: No student discount — Notion AI on the free student plan is comparable at lower cost
tools.uniHack: Reflect's networked note graph is excellent for connecting ideas across courses and semesters. The AI writing assistant helps draft summaries of complex topics in your own words — essential for deep learning.
tools.description: Reflect combines the networked note philosophy of Roam Research with a beautiful interface and AI writing assistant. Its end-to-end encryption and privacy focus make it popular among users who want intelligent note-taking without data exposure.

tools.name: Cleanvoice AI
tools.tagline: AI removes filler words and silences from podcasts
tools.bestFor: Podcast editing, audio cleanup, filler word removal
tools.studentPerk: Free 30-minute trial covers most short student project recordings
tools.uniHack: Remove all 'um', 'uh', 'like', and 'you know' from recorded presentations before submission. Instantly sounds more professional without manual editing. Game-changer for assessed presentations.
tools.description: Cleanvoice specialises in one thing: removing the audio artifacts that make recordings sound unpolished. Filler words, dead air, mouth sounds, and stutters are eliminated automatically — producing near-professional audio from casual recordings.

tools.name: Typeface
tools.tagline: Enterprise AI content platform with brand guardrails
tools.bestFor: Enterprise brand content, marketing scale, compliance
tools.studentPerk: Enterprise only — not suitable for individual students
tools.uniHack: Marketing and brand strategy students can explore Typeface's case studies and blog to understand how enterprise brand AI systems are architected — valuable industry knowledge.
tools.description: Typeface is purpose-built for enterprise brand content at scale, with sophisticated brand persona training and compliance guardrails. Backed by former Adobe CEO Shantanu Narayen, it targets regulated industries with strict content requirements.

tools.name: Harvey AI
tools.tagline: AI for legal work — contracts, research, and litigation
tools.bestFor: Legal research, contract review, litigation support
tools.studentPerk: Law firms using Harvey may offer access — check with your firm during internship or clerkship
tools.uniHack: Law students: understand Harvey's capabilities by reviewing their published case studies. Many Big Law firms now use Harvey — familiarity with AI legal tools is a growing hiring differentiator.
tools.description: Harvey is the leading AI platform for legal work, used by most major law firms globally. Built on a fine-tuned legal foundation model, it assists with contract review, research, due diligence, and litigation preparation with legal-grade accuracy.

tools.name: Playground AI
tools.tagline: Free image generation with professional editing tools
tools.bestFor: Free high-volume image generation, mixed real+AI editing
tools.studentPerk: Free tier generates 100 images per day — among the most generous free tiers available
tools.uniHack: 100 free images/day is enough for any design or media coursework. Playground's canvas editor combines AI generation with standard photo editing tools in one interface.
tools.description: Playground AI offers the most generous free tier in AI image generation at 100 images per day. Its canvas editor is unique in combining generated and real images in the same workspace, enabling sophisticated mixed-media compositions.

tools.name: Codeium
tools.tagline: Free AI code completion for individual developers
tools.bestFor: Free unlimited code completion, privacy options
tools.studentPerk: Completely free for individual use with unlimited completions — no student verification required
tools.uniHack: Free, unlimited code completions across 70+ languages and IDEs. The best free alternative to GitHub Copilot for students who don't qualify for or want the Student Developer Pack.
tools.description: Codeium provides unlimited free AI code completion for individual developers across 70+ programming languages and all major IDEs. Its team also built Windsurf — Codeium is the free tier, Windsurf the premium editor experience.

tools.name: Wordtune
tools.tagline: AI sentence rewriting and reading companion
tools.bestFor: Sentence rewriting, tone shifting, reading summaries
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 10 rewrites/day plus reading summaries — useful for paraphrasing sources in essays
tools.uniHack: Use Wordtune's Read & Summarize to get the key points of long articles in seconds, then use the rewrite tool to paraphrase quotes from sources in your own voice for essays — helps avoid accidental plagiarism.
tools.description: Wordtune from AI21 Labs specialises in rewriting at the sentence level, offering multiple rewrite variants with different tones and styles. Its Read & Summarize feature quickly distills long articles to their essential points.

tools.name: Merlin AI
tools.tagline: ChatGPT-powered browser assistant everywhere you browse
tools.bestFor: Summarising web pages, YouTube, browsing assistance
tools.studentPerk: Free tier gives 102 queries/day — enough for daily research and browsing assistance
tools.uniHack: Summarise any webpage, YouTube lecture, or research article directly in the browser with Cmd+M. Combine with Liner for a complete in-browser research workflow without switching apps.
tools.description: Merlin brings GPT-4 access to any webpage or application through a keyboard shortcut. Its YouTube summarisation is particularly popular for students who want the key points of long lecture videos without watching in full.

tools.name: Khanmigo
tools.tagline: AI tutor that teaches — never gives answers directly
tools.bestFor: Maths, science, history tutoring for K-12 and beyond
tools.studentPerk: $4/month — the most affordable dedicated AI tutoring platform. Designed specifically for student learning.
tools.uniHack: Khanmigo's Socratic approach means it guides you to the answer through questions rather than giving it — building deeper understanding than any other AI tool. Ideal for STEM subjects at $4/mo.
tools.description: Khanmigo is AI tutoring done right — built on pedagogical principles that prevent AI-dependence. Its Socratic method guides students to understanding through questions rather than providing answers, genuinely building knowledge rather than bypassing learning.

tools.name: Humata AI
tools.tagline: Chat with your PDF files and research documents
tools.bestFor: PDF Q&A, research paper analysis, document summarisation
tools.studentPerk: Student plan at $1.99/mo is one of the cheapest AI subscriptions available — excellent value for PDF-heavy courses
tools.uniHack: Upload dense research papers and ask questions directly: 'What methodology did they use?', 'What were the limitations?', 'Explain equation 3 in simple terms.' Cuts paper analysis time by 80%.
tools.description: Humata AI specialises in document intelligence — answering questions about uploaded PDFs with citations to the exact passage supporting each answer. Its student pricing tier makes it uniquely accessible for academic use.

tools.name: Taskade AI
tools.tagline: AI-powered project management and team workspace
tools.bestFor: Team projects, task management, AI-assisted planning
tools.studentPerk: Free plan covers unlimited tasks and projects — excellent for group coursework management
tools.uniHack: Create a group project workspace and use the AI to auto-generate project plans, meeting agendas, and progress reports from brief descriptions. Free for unlimited collaborators.
tools.description: Taskade combines AI-powered project generation with collaborative task management. Its AI can create complete project plans, meeting templates, and structured workflows from a brief description — making it faster than any manual project planning approach.

tools.name: Pi by Inflection
tools.tagline: Personal AI with emotional intelligence and warmth
tools.bestFor: Personal conversations, emotional support, daily check-ins
tools.studentPerk: Completely free — one of the few frontier-quality conversational AIs with no usage limits
tools.uniHack: Use Pi as a thinking partner for processing difficult ideas, brainstorming essay arguments, or working through complex decisions. Its conversational depth and patience make it excellent for reflective thinking during stressful periods.
tools.description: Pi by Inflection was built to be a genuinely caring personal AI companion. Its emotional intelligence, patience, and conversational warmth are distinct from task-focused AI assistants. Fully free with no usage limits, Pi has retained a loyal user base.
